edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback tail harvester 8:1 just hit ACCEPT (PF=1.442, 156 trades, DD=7.1%) — but the entry parameters were never varied. Every RSI pullback spec uses the DEFAULT rsi_long_max=40 and ema_slope=50. This spec tests TWO untouched parameter dimensions: (1) RSI depth threshold — does requiring DEEPER pullbacks (rsi_long_max=35) produce higher-quality entries at the cost of trade count? Does SHALLOWER entry (rsi_long_max=45) maintain edge while increasing participation? (2) EMA slope period — the template uses EMA_50 slope to confirm trend direction. Switching to EMA_21 slope (faster, more responsive) should capture shorter momentum swings and produce more signals. All three variants use 7:1 R:R (1.0x stop / 7.0x TP), filling the gap between the untested 5:1 and the proven 8:1 data points on the RSI pullback R:R curve. The tail harvester mechanics (tight stop, extreme R:R, low win rate) are proven. The question is whether entry SELECTIVITY matters — and if so, which direction (deeper vs shallower RSI, faster vs default slope). Regime assumption: ranging-dominant (8:1 ACCEPT showed ranging PF=1.795 >> trending PF=1.505). Invalidation: (1) if all three variants produce PF within 0.05 of each other, entry parameter optimization is irrelevant for RSI pullback — the tail harvester exits dominate regardless of entry quality; (2) if deeper RSI (35) produces PF > 1.5 with < 80 trades, there is a clear quality-over-quantity trade-off to exploit; (3) if fast slope (EMA_21) produces significantly more trades at similar PF, slope responsiveness is a free lever.

variants.name: rsi_deep_dip_tail_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with DEEPENED RSI threshold (rsi_long_max=35 vs default 40). Requires RSI to drop to 35 or below before entry — a genuine oversold pullback within an uptrend, not just a mild retracement. Paired with 7:1 R:R tail harvester exits (1.0x ATR stop / 7.0x ATR TP). The hypothesis: deeper pullbacks = higher probability of mean-reversion bounce = higher TP hit rate. Expected: fewer trades than the 156-trade 8:1 ACCEPT (maybe 80-120), but potentially higher PF if entry quality compensates for reduced participation.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope rising (EMA_50 current > EMA_50 previous bar).
RSI_14 <= 35.0 (deep pullback — genuine oversold within uptrend).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope falling (EMA_50 current < EMA_50 previous bar).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis: deep RSI dips (<=35) have higher mean-reversion probability, compensating for fewer trades
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — standard RSI pullback baseline
RSI_14 <= 35 (deepened) — requiring genuine oversold pullback within trend for higher-quality entries
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — tail harvester at 7:1 R:R, filling curve between 5:1 and 8:1

variants.name: rsi_shallow_dip_tail_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with RELAXED RSI threshold (rsi_long_max=45 vs default 40). Accepts shallower pullbacks — RSI only needs to dip to 45 (barely below neutral) within an uptrend. This dramatically increases the entry window: any mild retracement during an uptrend qualifies. The trade-off: more signals = more trades, but lower per-trade quality. Combined with 7:1 tail harvester exits, the question is whether the volume of trades compensates for lower entry selectivity. Expected: significantly more trades (200+), with PF depending on whether shallow RSI dips still produce enough 7x ATR moves to be profitable.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope rising (EMA_50 current > EMA_50 previous bar).
RSI_14 <= 45.0 (shallow pullback — mild retracement within uptrend).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope falling (EMA_50 current < EMA_50 previous bar).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis: relaxed RSI (45) maintains edge via volume — more trades × tail harvester = consistent returns
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — standard RSI pullback baseline
RSI_14 <= 45 (relaxed) — shallower pullback requirement for maximum trade count
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — tail harvester at 7:1 R:R, testing volume vs selectivity

variants.name: rsi_fast_slope_tail_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with FAST slope confirmation (ema_slope=21 vs default 50). Uses EMA_21 instead of EMA_50 for slope direction, making the trend confirmation more responsive to short-term momentum changes. Default RSI threshold (rsi_long_max=40). The hypothesis: EMA_21 slope captures intra-week momentum that EMA_50 misses — when a 21-bar EMA turns up AND RSI is oversold, the pullback is happening within a fresh short-term upswing, not just a broader trend. This should produce more entry signals (EMA_21 changes slope more often than EMA_50) with different timing characteristics. Combined with 7:1 tail harvester to isolate the slope-period effect from exit mechanics.
entry_long: EMA_21 slope rising (EMA_21 current > EMA_21 previous bar — fast slope confirmation).
entry_short: EMA_21 slope falling (EMA_21 current < EMA_21 previous bar — fast slope confirmation).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
EMA slope period: 21 (fast — more responsive than default 50)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis: faster EMA_21 slope produces more responsive entries — if PF holds, slope sensitivity is a free trade-count lever
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_21 slope (fast) — responsive slope catches intra-week momentum shifts
RSI_14 <= 40 (default) — standard pullback threshold, isolating the slope-period variable
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — tail harvester at 7:1 R:R, consistent exit structure across all variants
